meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Build a Portfolio: Automated Email System (Intermediate Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about a lack of experience. Learn to build an automated email system using Antigravity and Claude Code to showcase your engineering skills.
content.answer_block: Transform your resume by building an automated email engine. By integrating Antigravity for workflow orchestration and Claude Code for rapid development, you demonstrate real-world engineering proficiency. This project proves you can automate complex business logic, turning a 'no portfolio' gap into a high-value technical asset for recruiters.
content.intro:
The blank page of your portfolio is a silent killer of career momentum. While your peers chase theoretical certifications, you are trapped in the 'tutorial hell' loop, watching videos instead of shipping products. The industry doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about your ability to solve messy, real-world problems.

Today, we break that cycle. We are building a production-grade email automation system. This isn't a toy script; it is a scalable architecture that demonstrates you understand event-driven design, API integration, and automated deployments. By the end of this, you won't just have a project—you'll have a narrative.

steps.title: Define the Architecture
steps.description: Establish the blueprint for your automation engine, focusing on modularity and error handling before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Design a robust, scalable email automation pipeline that uses a queue-based system to handle rate-limiting and retries. Provide a Mermaid diagram and a high-level folder structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md outlining the email automation architecture using a queue-based system.'

steps.title: Configure the Environment
steps.description: Standardize your dev environment to ensure your code is production-ready and easily reproducible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure .env schema and a robust configuration handler for handling API keys for SendGrid and SMTP providers, ensuring sensitive data is never hardcoded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a secure config manager and .env.example file for the project.'

steps.title: Implement the Email Engine
steps.description: Write the core logic for sending emails, emphasizing clean, testable code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop an email service module that implements a retry strategy and logging. Use an interface-driven approach so we can swap SMTP providers later.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate an email service class with retry logic and unit tests using pytest.'

steps.title: Build the Trigger Logic
steps.description: Define the events that trigger your automation, simulating a real-world SaaS workflow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a trigger handler that listens for simulated webhook events and passes them to our email service. Ensure it handles malformed payloads gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the webhook trigger handler and add input validation using Pydantic.'

steps.title: Final Testing & Deployment Prep
steps.description: Verify your system with comprehensive integration tests to ensure reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests that verify the full flow: trigger to email dispatch. Include a mock server setup for testing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write integration tests for the full pipeline and ensure all tests pass in the CLI.'

pro_tips: Always implement exponential backoff for API retries to prevent getting blacklisted by email providers.
Use structured logging (JSON format) from day one; it makes debugging production issues 10x faster.
Never store API keys in your repository; use a local .env file and document the required variables in your README.
Write 'Contract Tests' between your modules so that a change in the email engine doesn't break your trigger logic.
Document the 'Why' behind your architectural choices in your portfolio repo—recruiters value decision-making over just raw code.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a free tier platform like Render or Railway, then write a short blog post on your portfolio site explaining the challenges you solved during the build.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn a blank portfolio into a showcase of automated engineering. Stop waiting for permission to build. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
